schools.summary: The lot of the children of Joseph, the portion of the favoured son fulfilled in his sons: 'and the lot of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan by Jericho, unto the water of Jericho on the east, to the wilderness that goeth up from Jericho throughout mount Bethel' — the portion of Joseph — 'and the lot of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan by Jericho, unto the water of Jericho on the east, to the wilderness that goeth up from Jericho throughout mount Bethel': the children of Joseph, Ephraim and Manasseh, received their lot, the portion of the favoured son (the witnesses noting the significance — the inheritance of Joseph, the son who received the birthright forfeited by Reuben, now realised in his two tribes, the double portion of the firstborn, Joseph given the birthright and the double portion in his two sons, the blessing of Jacob fulfilled, 'the birthright was Joseph's,' 1 Chronicles 5:2, the lesson — that God honoured his promise to Joseph, the dreamer sold into Egypt, now exalted in his sons to a double inheritance, the providence that turned his affliction to honour, 'God meant it unto good,' Genesis 50:20, the truth that the blessing of Jacob upon Joseph, 'a fruitful bough... whose branches run over the wall,' Genesis 49:22, was fulfilled in the spreading inheritance of Ephraim and Manasseh, the figure — the reward of the faithful sufferer exalted, Joseph a type of Christ, sold and yet exalted to give bread and inheritance to his brethren, the truth that God exalts the humbled and gives the double portion to the faithful, the figure of the inheritance of the birthright, the blessing realised in the sons, the call to trust the God who turns affliction to honour, the assurance that God fulfils his promise to the faithful, the figure of the inheritance of the birthright, the blessing of Joseph realised in Ephraim and Manasseh); the children of Joseph receiving their lot from Jordan by Jericho, the portion of the favoured son assigned (the inheritance of Joseph, 1 Chronicles 5:2) — the lot (of Joseph); 'to the wilderness that goeth up from Jericho throughout mount Bethel' (the border of Joseph's portion running through mount Bethel, the blessing of Jacob upon Joseph fulfilled in the spreading inheritance, Joseph a type of Christ exalted, the blessing realised, Genesis 49:22; Genesis 50:20) — the figure (the reward of the faithful sufferer exalted, the providence, the lot, the border of Ephraim to follow, vv.2-4) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the lot of the children of Joseph signify?

sides.position: The inheritance of the birthright, the blessing of Joseph realised in Ephraim and Manasseh - the portion of the son who received the birthright forfeited by Reuben, the double portion of the firstborn (1 Chronicles 5:2), God honouring his promise to Joseph the dreamer sold into Egypt and exalted (Genesis 50:20), the blessing of Jacob 'a fruitful bough' fulfilled (Genesis 49:22), Joseph a type of Christ exalted to give inheritance to his brethren

themes: And the lot of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan by Jericho, unto the water of Jericho on the east, to the wilderness that goeth up from Jericho throughout mount Bethel
the lot of the children of Joseph, the portion of the favoured son (the crux of the inheritance of the birthright realised in Ephraim and Manasseh)
the inheritance of the son who received the birthright forfeited by Reuben, realised in his two tribes, the double portion of the firstborn
God honouring his promise to Joseph the dreamer, the blessing of Jacob fulfilled, Joseph a type of Christ sold and yet exalted (1 Chronicles 5:2; Genesis 50:20; Genesis 49:22)

schools.summary: The border of Joseph from Bethel to Ataroth: 'and goeth out from Bethel to Luz, and passeth along unto the borders of Archi to Ataroth' — the border of Joseph ran from Bethel by Luz to Ataroth (the and goeth out from Bethel to Luz, from Bethel to Luz, the and passeth along unto the borders of Archi to Ataroth, to Ataroth, the figure, the border of the inheritance traced by its landmarks, the portion bounded, the truth that the line of Joseph's portion was marked from place to place, the figure of the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the portion defined, the border to Ataroth, the inheritance marked, vv.1); the border from Bethel to Luz (the line) — the line (from Bethel); 'passeth along unto the borders of Archi to Ataroth' (the border to Ataroth, the landmark, the figure, the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the providence, the line, the border to the Japhleti and Bethhoron to follow, vv.3) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And goeth out from Bethel to Luz, and passeth along unto the borders of Archi to Ataroth
the border of Joseph from Bethel to Ataroth
the border of Joseph running from Bethel by Luz to Ataroth
the portion marked by its landmarks

schools.summary: The border of Joseph completing at the sea by Bethhoron and Gezer: 'and goeth down westward to the coast of Japhleti, unto the coast of Bethhoron the nether, and to Gezer: and the goings out thereof are at the sea' — the border ran down by Bethhoron and Gezer to the sea (the and goeth down westward to the coast of Japhleti, unto the coast of Bethhoron the nether, by Bethhoron, the and to Gezer: and the goings out thereof are at the sea, to Gezer and the sea, the figure, the border completed at the sea, the portion fully bounded, Gezer named that would not be cleared, the truth that the line of Joseph reached the sea, the portion defined, the figure of the inheritance bounded to the sea, Gezer the flaw to come, the border at the sea, the inheritance marked, vv.2); the border by Bethhoron (the line) — the line (by Bethhoron); 'to Gezer: and the goings out thereof are at the sea' (the border to Gezer and the sea, the bound, the figure, the inheritance bounded to the sea, the providence, the border, the inheritance of Ephraim and Manasseh to follow, vv.4) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And goeth down westward to the coast of Japhleti, unto the coast of Bethhoron the nether, and to Gezer: and the goings out thereof are at the sea

schools.summary: The children of Joseph taking their inheritance: 'so the children of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, took their inheritance' — Manasseh and Ephraim, the two tribes of Joseph, took their possession (the so the children of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, took their inheritance, the inheritance taken, the figure, the two tribes of Joseph possessing the double portion of the firstborn, the truth that Manasseh and Ephraim, the sons of Joseph, took their inheritance, the birthright realised, the figure of the double portion possessed, the blessing of the firstborn fulfilled, 'the birthright was Joseph's,' 1 Chronicles 5:2, the inheritance of Joseph taken, the portion possessed, vv.1); Manasseh and Ephraim, the two tribes of Joseph (the tribes, 1 Chronicles 5:2) — the tribes (Manasseh and Ephraim); 'took their inheritance' (the inheritance possessed, the portion, the figure, the double portion possessed, the providence, the possession, the particular border of Ephraim to follow, vv.5) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The east border of Ephraim by Atarothaddar and Bethhoron: 'and the border of the children of Ephraim according to their families was thus: even the border of their inheritance on the east side was Atarothaddar, unto Bethhoron the upper' — the east border of Ephraim ran by Atarothaddar to upper Bethhoron (the and the border of the children of Ephraim according to their families was thus, the border of Ephraim, the even the border of their inheritance on the east side was Atarothaddar, unto Bethhoron the upper, by Atarothaddar to Bethhoron, the figure, the border of Ephraim traced by its landmarks, the portion bounded, the truth that the line of Ephraim's portion was marked by its towns, the figure of the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the portion defined, the border to Bethhoron, the inheritance marked, vv.1); the border of Ephraim by families (the portion) — the border (of Ephraim); 'the border of their inheritance on the east side was Atarothaddar, unto Bethhoron the upper' (the east border to Bethhoron, the landmark, the figure, the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the providence, the border, the border to the sea and Michmethah to follow, vv.6) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And the border of the children of Ephraim according to their families was thus: even the border of their inheritance on the east side was Atarothaddar, unto Bethhoron the upper

schools.summary: The border of Ephraim by Michmethah and Taanathshiloh: 'and the border went out toward the sea to Michmethah on the north side; and the border went about eastward unto Taanathshiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janohah' — the border ran by Michmethah and Taanathshiloh to Janohah (the and the border went out toward the sea to Michmethah on the north side, by Michmethah, the and the border went about eastward unto Taanathshiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janohah, to Janohah, the figure, the border traced by its landmarks, the portion bounded, the truth that the line of Ephraim was marked from town to town, the figure of the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the portion defined, the border to Janohah, the inheritance marked, vv.5); the border by Michmethah (the line) — the line (by Michmethah); 'the border went about eastward unto Taanathshiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janohah' (the border to Janohah, the landmark, the figure, the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the providence, the line, the border down to Ataroth and Jericho to follow, vv.7) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And the border went out toward the sea to Michmethah on the north side; and the border went about eastward unto Taanathshiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janohah

schools.summary: The border of Ephraim down to Ataroth, Naarath, and Jericho: 'and it went down from Janohah to Ataroth, and to Naarath, and came to Jericho, and went out at Jordan' — the border ran down by Ataroth and Naarath to Jericho and Jordan (the and it went down from Janohah to Ataroth, and to Naarath, by Ataroth and Naarath, the and came to Jericho, and went out at Jordan, to Jericho and Jordan, the figure, the border traced down to Jordan, the portion bounded, the truth that the line of Ephraim ended at Jordan, the figure of the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the portion defined, the border to Jordan, the inheritance marked, vv.6); the border by Ataroth and Naarath (the line) — the line (by Ataroth); 'came to Jericho, and went out at Jordan' (the border to Jericho and Jordan, the bound, the figure, the inheritance bounded by its landmarks, the providence, the line, the border from Tappuah to the sea to follow, vv.8) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The border of Ephraim completing at the sea, the inheritance of the tribe: 'the border went out from Tappuah westward unto the river Kanah; and the goings out thereof were at the sea. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families' — the border ran from Tappuah by the river Kanah to the sea, completing Ephraim's inheritance (the the border went out from Tappuah westward unto the river Kanah; and the goings out thereof were at the sea, to the sea, the This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families, the inheritance of Ephraim, the figure, the border completed at the sea, the inheritance of Ephraim fully bounded, the truth that the portion of Ephraim was traced to its end at the sea, the figure of the inheritance fully bounded and possessed, the portion of God's appointment, the inheritance of Ephraim defined, the portion possessed, vv.5); the border by the river Kanah to the sea (the bound) — the bound (to the sea); 'This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families' (the inheritance of Ephraim, the portion, the figure, the inheritance fully bounded and possessed, the providence, the inheritance, the cities of Ephraim within Manasseh to follow, vv.9) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The separate cities of Ephraim within Manasseh's inheritance: 'and the separate cities for the children of Ephraim were among the inheritance of the children of Manasseh, all the cities with their villages' — certain cities of Ephraim lay within Manasseh's portion (the and the separate cities for the children of Ephraim were among the inheritance of the children of Manasseh, the separate cities, the all the cities with their villages, with their villages, the figure, the cities of Ephraim set within Manasseh, the tribes intermingled, the truth that Ephraim had cities within Manasseh's portion, the brethren joined, the figure of the tribes mingled in the inheritance, the unity of the brethren, the cities of Ephraim within Manasseh, the portions joined, vv.8); the cities of Ephraim within Manasseh (the cities) — the cities (within Manasseh); 'all the cities with their villages' (the cities with their villages, the portion, the figure, the tribes mingled in the inheritance, the providence, the cities, the Canaanites of Gezer not driven out to follow, vv.10) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And the separate cities for the children of Ephraim were among the inheritance of the children of Manasseh, all the cities with their villages

schools.summary: That Ephraim drove not out the Canaanites of Gezer, but put them to tribute: 'and they drave not out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer: but the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites unto this day, and serve under tribute' — the incomplete obedience — 'and they drave not out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer: but the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites unto this day, and serve under tribute': Ephraim failed to drive out the Canaanites of Gezer as God commanded, but spared them for tribute (the witnesses noting the significance — the failure of full obedience, the Canaanites left in the land contrary to God's command, spared for the gain of tribute rather than destroyed, the command to drive out wholly disobeyed, the enemy left for the love of gain, the compromise with the world that God had forbidden, 'thou shalt utterly drive them out... lest they teach you to do after all their abominations,' Deuteronomy 20:17-18, the lesson — that incomplete obedience, sparing what God commands to be destroyed, leaves a snare in the midst of God's people, the Canaanites a thorn that would later seduce them to idolatry, 'they shall be as thorns in your sides, and their gods shall be a snare unto you,' Judges 2:3, the truth that the world spared for gain becomes a trap, the sin tolerated a corruption, the compromise that seems profitable proving ruinous, the figure — the besetting sin spared, the corruption left in the heart that should be wholly put off, 'mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth,' Colossians 3:5, the truth that to spare the enemy for advantage is to keep a snare, the unmortified sin that festers, the figure of the half-hearted obedience that tolerates what God condemns, the call to drive out the enemy wholly, sparing nothing God has condemned, the warning that the tolerated sin becomes a snare and a scourge, the figure of the incomplete obedience that leaves the enemy in the land, the compromise with the world that becomes a snare); Ephraim failing to drive out the Canaanites of Gezer as commanded, the full obedience left undone (the incomplete obedience, Deuteronomy 20:17-18) — the failure (drave not out); 'but the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites unto this day, and serve under tribute' (the Canaanites spared for tribute, left dwelling among Ephraim, the enemy tolerated for gain becoming a snare to seduce them to idolatry, the compromise with the world, Judges 2:3; Colossians 3:5) — the figure (the besetting sin spared, the corruption left that should be put off, the providence, the failure, the inheritance of Manasseh to follow, ch.17) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does Ephraim's failure to drive out the Canaanites of Gezer signify?

sides.position: The incomplete obedience that leaves the enemy in the land, the compromise with the world that becomes a snare - the failure of full obedience, the Canaanites spared for the gain of tribute against God's command (Deuteronomy 20:17-18), the enemy a snare that would seduce God's people to idolatry (Judges 2:3), a figure of the besetting sin tolerated for advantage that should be wholly mortified (Colossians 3:5)

themes: And they drave not out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer: but the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites unto this day, and serve under tribute
that Ephraim drove not out the Canaanites of Gezer, but put them to tribute (the crux of the incomplete obedience that leaves the enemy in the land)
the failure of full obedience, the enemy left in the land contrary to God's command, spared for the gain of tribute rather than destroyed
the Canaanites a snare that would seduce God's people to idolatry, a figure of the besetting sin tolerated that should be wholly mortified (Deuteronomy 20:17-18; Judges 2:3; Colossians 3:5)
